scantool.ptxt: As the data values are standard values estimated on the basis of values obtained from the normally operating vehicles using a scan tool, use them as reference values. Even when the vehicle is in good condition, there may be cases where the checked value does not fall within each specified data range. Therefore, judgment as abnormal should not be made depending on these data alone.

Also, conditions that can be checked by the scan tool are those detected by ECM and output from ECM as commands and there may be cases where the engine or actuator is not operating (in the condition) as indicated by the scan tool. Be sure to use the timing light to check the ignition timing.

item.ptxt: With the CAN communication generic scan tool, only data marked with asterisk (*) in the following table can be read. (Euro-OBD model)

When checking data with engine running at idle or racing, be sure to place gear shift lever in “Neutral” (M/T model) or select “P” range (A/T model) and apply parking brake fully. Also, if nothing or “no load” is indicated, turn off A/C, all electric loads, P/S and all the other switches.

entry.ptxt: Open-loop control before satisfaction of closed-loop control conditions (ECT >10 °C, IAT, TP, HO2S-1/system = OK)
Open-loop control due to driving conditions (Power enrichment, etc.)
Open-loop control due to system fault detected
Closed-loop control using HO2S-1 as feedback sensors for fuel control
Closed-loop control due to system fault detected
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed with no load after warming up
Engine: Idle speed with no load after warming up
Engine: Idle speed with no load after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed with no load after warming up
Almost the same as speedometer reading
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed with no load after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: 2,000 rpm for 3 min. or more after warming up
Manual A/C: 2nd or higher speed position
Auto A/C: 6th or higher speed position
Manual A/C: Other than above condition
Auto A/C: Other than above condition
Shift select lever: Other than “P” or “N” position
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine condition: 2,000 rpm or more after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up
Engine: Idle speed after warming up

topic.title: Fuel System (Fuel system status):
topic.ptxt: A/F ratio feedback loop status is displayed as one of the followings.
OL: Open-loop control before satisfaction of closed loop control conditions (ECT > 10 °C, IAT, TP, HO2S-1/system = OK).
CL: Closed-loop control using oxygen sensor(s) as feedback sensor(s).
OL-DRIVE: Open-loop control due to driving requirement (power-up enrichment etc.).
OL-FAULT: Open-loop control due to system fault detected.
CL-FAULT: Closed-loop control due to system fault detected.

topic.title: Short Term Fuel Trim (%):
topic.ptxt: Short term fuel trim value represents short term corrections to air/fuel mixture. 0 (zero) indicates no correction, a value greater than 0 indicates enrichment correction, and a value less than 0 indicates enleanment correction.

topic.title: Long Term Fuel Trim (%):
topic.ptxt: Long term fuel trim value represents long term corrections to air/fuel mixture. 0 (zero) indicates no correction, a value greater than 0 indicates enrichment correction, and a value less than 0 indicates enleanment correction.

topic.title: Total Fuel Trim (%):
topic.ptxt: Total Fuel Trim is calculated from Short Term Fuel Trim and Long Term Fuel Trim. This value indicates how much correction is necessary to keep the air/fuel mixture stoichiometrical.

topic.title: Inj Pulse Width (Fuel injection pulse width, msec.):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the injector drive pulse width (time) during which the injector valve stays open as commanded by ECM. (For multipoint fuel injection, the injector drive time means that of the No. 1 cylinder.)

topic.title: Ignition Advance (Ignition timing advance for No.1 cylinder, ° BTDC):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the ignition timing of No.1 cylinder commanded by ECM. The actual ignition timing should be checked using the timing light.

topic.title: Calculated Load (Calculated load value, %):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates calculated engine load in percent. The load is calculated based on input signals to ECM from various engine sensors.

topic.title: Engine Speed (rpm):
topic.ptxt: The engine speed is calculated from reference pulses from the camshaft position sensor.

topic.title: Desired Idle (Desired idle speed, rpm):
topic.ptxt: The Desired Idle Speed is an ECM internal parameter which indicates the ECM requested idle speed. If the engine is not running, this number is not valid.

topic.title: MAF (Mass air flow rate, g/sec, lb/min.):
topic.ptxt: It represents total mass of air entering intake manifold which is measured by MAF sensor.

topic.title: Vehicle Speed (km/h, mph):
topic.ptxt: The vehicle speed is calculated based on pulse signals from wheel speed sensors.

topic.title: ECT (Engine coolant temperature, °C, °F):
topic.ptxt: It is detected by engine coolant temperature sensor.

topic.title: Intake Air Temperature. (°C, °F):
topic.ptxt: It is detected by intake air temperature sensor.

topic.title: Barometric Pres (Barometric pressure, kPa, mmHg, inHg, psi, inH2O):
topic.ptxt: This parameter represents barometric air pressure measurement and is used for altitude correction of the fuel injection rate.

topic.title: Evap Canist Prg Duty (Evap canister purge flow duty, %):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates valve ON (valve open) time rate within a certain set cycle of EVAP canister purge valve which controls the amount of EVAP purge.

topic.title: VVT Gap (Variable valve timing difference between target and actual position, °):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates difference between target camshaft position and actual camshaft position.

topic.title: IAC Throttle Opening (Idle air throttle valve opening, %):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates throttle valve opening of idle air control in % (100% indicates the maximum idle air flow).

topic.title: TP Sensor 1 Volt (TP sensor (main) output voltage, V):
topic.ptxt: TP Sensor (Main) data provides throttle valve opening information in the form of voltage.

topic.title: TP Sensor 2 Volt (TP sensor (sub) output voltage, V):
topic.ptxt: TP Sensor (Sub) data provides throttle valve opening information in the form of voltage.

topic.title: APP Sensor 1 Volt (APP sensor (main) output voltage, V):
topic.ptxt: APP Sensor (Main) data provides accelerator pedal position information in the form of voltage.

topic.title: APP Sensor 2 Volt (APP sensor (sub) output voltage, V):
topic.ptxt: APP Sensor (Sub) data provides accelerator pedal position information in the form of voltage.

topic.title: Accel Position (Absolute accelerator pedal position, %):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates calculated accelerator pedal position in percent based on input from accelerator pedal position sensor.

topic.title: Throttle Position (Absolute throttle position, %):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates calculated throttle opening in percent based on input from throttle position sensor.

topic.title: Target Throttle Posi (Target throttle valve position, %):
topic.ptxt: Target Throttle Valve Position is an ECM internal parameter which indicates the ECM requested throttle valve position.

topic.title: O2S B1 S1 (HO2S-1, V):
topic.ptxt: It indicates output voltage of HO2S-1 installed on exhaust manifold (Pre-TWC).

topic.title: O2S B1 S2 (HO2S-2, V):
topic.ptxt: It indicates output voltage of HO2S-2 installed on exhaust manifold (post-TWC). It is used to detect catalyst deterioration.

topic.title: Battery Voltage (V):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates battery voltage inputted from main relay to ECM.

topic.title: Fuel Level (%):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates approximate fuel level in the fuel tank. The fuel level data is provided in percentage from 0 to 100%.

topic.title: A/C Pressure (A/C refrigerant absolute pressure, kPa):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates A/C refrigerant absolute pressure detected by A/C refrigerant pressure sensor.

topic.title: Radiator Fan (Radiator cooling fan control relay, Low/High/Off):
topic.ptxt: Low: ON command is being output to the radiator cooling fan relay No.1.
High: ON command is being output to radiator cooling fan relays No.2 and No.3.
Off: No command is being output.

topic.title: A/C Comp Relay (A/C compressor relay, ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the state of the A/C compressor relay.

topic.title: Fuel Pump (ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: ON is displayed when ECM activates the fuel pump via the fuel pump relay switch.

topic.title: Starter SW (Starter switch, ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates state of starting motor relay output.
ON: Starting motor relay is ON.
OFF: Starting motor relay is OFF.

topic.title: A/C Switch (ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: ON: Command for A/C operation is being output from ECM to HVAC.
OFF: Command for A/C operation is not being output.

topic.title: Blower Fan (ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the state of the blower speed selector.

topic.title: Brake Switch (ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the state of the brake switch.

topic.title: Electric Load (ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: ON: ON signal of headlight, clearance lights or rear end door window defogger is input.
OFF: Above electric loads all turned off.

topic.title: PNP Signal (P/N or D range) (A/T model):
topic.ptxt: Whether the transmission range switch is in “P” or “N” range or other ranges is displayed. If it is in “P” or “N” range, “P/N” is displayed and if it is in other than “P” and “N” range, “D” is displayed.

topic.title: Fuel Cut (ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: ON: Fuel injection is cut (output signal to injector is stopped).
OFF: Fuel injection is not cut.

topic.title: Closed Throttle Pos (Closed throttle position, ON/OFF):
topic.ptxt: This parameter is ON when throttle valve is at idle position and OFF when the valve is not at idle position.

topic.title: O2S B1 S1 ACT (HO2S-1, Active / Inactive):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the activation condition of HO2S-1.
ACTIVE: The sensor is activated.
INACTIVE: The sensor is being warmed up or not activated.

topic.title: O2S B1 S2 ACT (HO2S-2, Active / Inactive):
topic.ptxt: This parameter indicates the activation condition of HO2S-2.
ACTIVE: The sensor is activated.
INACTIVE: The sensor is being warmed up or not activated.
